name: Jefferson County Teachers
description_short: Jefferson County Teachers Credit Union is a small Florida-based credit union serving educators with $14M in assets, one branch, and a well-capitalized financial position.
description_long:
Jefferson County Teachers Credit Union was chartered in 1964 and operates as a state-chartered credit union headquartered in Monticello, Florida. It serves a membership base of 1,245 members, primarily educators in the Jefferson County area, with a field of membership restricted to teachers and qualifying individuals. The credit union maintains a single branch location and minimal staffing of 3 full-time employees and no part-time employees, reflecting its small-community focus.

The credit union offers traditional banking services including savings accounts, share deposits, auto loans, mortgages, and personal loans. As of Q1 2024, it holds $14.06M in total assets with $12.01M in member shares and deposits. The organization provides basic lending services, with $10.67M in total loans outstanding as of the latest reporting period. Members can access their accounts through traditional branch banking at their single Monticello location, though the credit union operates zero ATMs.

Jefferson County Teachers CU is distinguished by its deep local roots, educator-focused mission, and exceptionally strong financial health. With a net worth ratio of 14.59, the credit union is classified as "well-capitalized" — significantly above the 7.0 threshold required for that designation. The organization maintains conservative underwriting practices, with $228K in loan loss allowances against its $10.67M loan portfolio. This financial strength suggests stable, secure banking for its members despite its minimal size and single-branch footprint.

The primary caveat for prospective members is the credit union's extremely limited physical presence and operational capacity. With zero ATMs and only one branch in a small rural Florida county, accessibility is significantly restricted compared to larger financial institutions. The institution's small membership base and minimal staff suggest limited service hours, slower processing times, and potentially fewer product options than regional credit unions. This credit union is suitable only for local members or those with strong ties to Jefferson County. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Auto loans (new and used vehicle financing)
Loan products with credit loss expense management
Basic deposit insurance through NCUA (National Credit Union Administration)
Member account services at single branch location
Limited non-interest fee income services (overdraft fees, miscellaneous fees)
pros: Well-capitalized with net worth ratio of 14.59, well above the 7.0 minimum threshold for financial strength
Established in 1964 with 60+ years of operational history and stability
Conservative lending practices with substantial loan loss allowances ($228K for $10.67M portfolio)
Tax-exempt credit union structure, potentially offering lower fees than banks
Membership specifically designed for educators, creating targeted community focus
Positive net income of $32,025 in Q1 2024 despite small scale
Member-owned cooperative structure with democratic governance
cons: Zero ATMs available, requiring in-person branch visits for cash access
Single branch location in Monticello, FL, making accessibility extremely limited for non-local members
Only 3 full-time employees and minimal staffing suggest very limited service hours and transaction processing speed
Small asset base ($14M) may limit product variety, loan amounts, and technology features compared to larger institutions
Highly restrictive field of membership based on educator status, excluding most consumers
best_for: Local educators and school employees in Jefferson County, Florida with need for basic banking services
Teachers seeking community-based credit union with strong capital reserves and educator-focused mission
Members with flexible scheduling who can visit the single Monticello branch during operating hours
Individuals prioritizing financial stability and conservative underwriting over service convenience
diagnosis: Jefferson County Teachers Credit Union is appropriate exclusively for local educators in Jefferson County, Florida who can access the single Monticello branch and require basic banking services. The primary caveat is severe geographic and accessibility limitations—with zero ATMs, one branch, and only 3 employees—making this unsuitable for anyone without local presence or those expecting modern banking convenience.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
